message:
fix(btrfs): reject inline ram_bytes mismatches

 {"id":"bd-8fpo","title":"ffs-cli evidence streaming parser fails on non-UTF8 torn lines","description":"Fresh-eyes audit found behavior regression: load_evidence_records used BufRead::lines and could return InvalidData on non-UTF8 corrupted lines, unlike prior tolerant parser behavior. Fixed by byte-wise read_until + UTF-8 fallible decode + skip malformed lines; added regression test.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-02-23T01:41:55.499849552Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-23T01:41:55.499849552Z","closed_at":"2026-02-23T01:41:55.499849552Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","reliability"]}
 {"id":"bd-8ix3b","title":"Freeze exact scrub finding/report display contracts","description":"Tighten substring-only ScrubFinding and ScrubReport display tests in ffs-repair to exact golden contracts, then validate through rch.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-19T05:55:45.772982861Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-19T06:04:52.458770835Z","closed_at":"2026-04-19T06:04:52.456401826Z","close_reason":"Froze substring-only ScrubFinding and ScrubReport display tests to exact golden contracts and validated with rch.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["ffs-repair","golden-artifacts","testing"]}
+{"id":"bd-8ixid","title":"Reject uncompressed btrfs inline ram_bytes mismatch","description":"parse_extent_data accepts uncompressed inline extents whose ram_bytes field disagrees with the actual inline payload length. Downstream read/materialization uses data.len() for uncompressed inline extents, so malformed metadata can expose bytes outside the declared logical extent instead of failing closed. Validate ram_bytes == inline payload length for compression=none, keep compressed inline semantics unchanged, and add regression coverage.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-30T22:02:41.911752035Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-30T22:12:42.472475031Z","closed_at":"2026-04-30T22:12:42.470566869Z","close_reason":"Rejected uncompressed btrfs inline extents whose ram_bytes disagree with payload length while preserving compressed inline sizing semantics; added regressions and validated with fmt, focused tests, workspace check, clippy, and UBS.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["btrfs","parser","reliability"]}
 {"id":"bd-8kia","title":"Guard mkfs output creation against TOCTOU","description":"mkfs_cmd_with_program checks output.exists() before File::create, which can be raced and truncates existing files. Use OpenOptions::create_new and add regression test that existing output is rejected without truncation.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-11T17:19:18.790469066Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-11T17:23:58.311898269Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T17:23:58.311637014Z","close_reason":"Use create_new for mkfs output, prevent truncation/TOCTOU, add regression test","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-8qrt","title":"Validate btrfs sys_chunk keys and mirror stripe counts","description":"Add validation for sys_chunk_array key type/objectid and enforce mirror-stripe count/device rules in logical mapping","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-11T12:35:40.426042231Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-11T12:47:03.822304554Z","closed_at":"2026-04-11T12:47:03.822099727Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-8rgp","title":"Add btrfs write-path FUSE E2E tests mirroring ext4 coverage","description":"## What
Add btrfs write-path FUSE E2E tests that exercise create/write/mkdir/rename/unlink/truncate/
xattr/fallocate/symlink through the actual FUSE mount interface, mirroring the ext4 FUSE E2E
tests that already exist in fuse_e2e.rs.

## Why
ext4 has comprehensive FUSE E2E tests: fuse_create_and_read_file, fuse_write_overwrite_and_append,
fuse_mkdir_and_nested_create, fuse_unlink_removes_file, fuse_rename_file, fuse_hard_link,
fuse_symlink_create_and_follow, fuse_setattr_truncate — all exercising real kernel VFS through
FUSE mount.

btrfs write operations are tested only at the unit level (OpenFs methods in ffs-core) and in
the crash matrix. There are NO btrfs FUSE E2E write tests that go through the kernel.

## What Already Exists
- ext4 FUSE E2E write tests in crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs (lines 531-673)
- btrfs FUSE E2E read tests exist (mount + read files)
- Helper: with_rw_mount() for ext4 RW FUSE mounts
- btrfs mkfs.btrfs image creation in fuse_e2e.rs (line 1889-1910)
- btrfs unit write tests in ffs-core (create, mkdir, rename, fallocate, etc.)

## Implementation Plan
Add to crates/ffs-harness/tests/fuse_e2e.rs, using a with_btrfs_rw_mount() helper:

fn fuse_btrfs_create_and_read_file()
  - mkfs.btrfs → mount --rw → create file → read back → verify content → unmount

fn fuse_btrfs_write_and_read_back()
  - mount → create → write 4KB → read → verify → unmount

fn fuse_btrfs_mkdir_and_nested_create()
  - mount → mkdir → create file in subdir → read → verify → unmount

fn fuse_btrfs_rename_file()
  - mount → create → rename → verify new path exists, old doesn't → unmount

fn fuse_btrfs_unlink_removes_file()
  - mount → create → unlink → verify gone → unmount

fn fuse_btrfs_setattr_truncate()
  - mount → create → write 8KB → truncate to 4KB → verify size → unmount

fn fuse_btrfs_xattr_set_get_list_remove()
  - mount → create file → setxattr → getxattr → listxattr → removexattr → unmount

fn fuse_btrfs_fallocate_prealloc()
  - mount → create → fallocate 4096 bytes → verify size → unmount

fn fuse_btrfs_symlink_create_and_follow()
  - mount → create target → symlink → readlink → read through symlink → unmount

### Helper: with_btrfs_rw_mount()
- Create 256MB sparse file
- mkfs.btrfs -f on it
- Mount via FrankenFS FUSE with --rw
- Run test closure
- Unmount
- Skip if mkfs.btrfs or /dev/fuse unavailable

## Considerations
- Tests need both mkfs.btrfs and /dev/fuse — skip gracefully if either missing
- btrfs minimum image ~128-256MB (much larger than ext4 8MB) — use sparse files
- Use --test-threads=1 for FUSE tests to avoid mount contention
- Tests must unmount cleanly even on assertion failure (use Drop guard)
- btrfs write support is experimental — some operations may not be fully wired
  through FUSE yet; tests should document any that return EOPNOTSUPP

## Test Plan
- cargo test -p ffs-harness --test fuse_e2e -- fuse_btrfs --nocapture --test-threads=1
- All tests pass when mkfs.btrfs and /dev/fuse available
- All tests skip cleanly when either is unavailable
- No interference with ext4 FUSE tests

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 9 btrfs FUSE E2E write-path tests implemented
- [ ] with_btrfs_rw_mount() helper handles setup/teardown
- [ ] Tests skip gracefully without mkfs.btrfs or /dev/fuse
- [ ] All 9 tests pass on systems with FUSE+btrfs-progs
- [ ] Unmount cleanup is reliable (Drop guard)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-04-06T18:46:46.038588133Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-06T21:23:42.513062175Z","closed_at":"2026-04-06T21:23:42.512793045Z","close_reason":"Already implemented: 19 btrfs FUSE E2E tests exist at fuse_e2e.rs:1965-2395 including create, mkdir, rename, unlink, symlink, xattr, fsync, large write","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
